decision: Use internal/service/config as the runtime configuration boundary while preserving setup.yaml and setup package loading as the underlying source.
reason: This removes direct route-level dependence on setup globals without forcing a broad domain-model rewrite, preserving current config values and external behavior.
impact: Container wiring, setup handlers, server route wiring, and admin user creation now read config through the container-owned service.

decision: Use internal/service/config as the primary runtime boundary while keeping backend/setup as the source compatibility layer.
reason: This preserves setup.yaml and existing config values while moving container and route wiring off backend/setup globals.
impact: Server startup, admin user creation, setup handlers, and stats now consume a container-owned config service.
